First thing you should know is that I come from very humble beginnings. 



My two siblings and I shared a bedroom when we were younger. 



However, my parents always made sure we had the necessities. 



I only say this to let you know that I didn't have a head start compared to my peers in 

regards to my success that I'm having right now. 



In fact, I went to less than one semester of junior college before dropping out, knowing it wasn't for me. 



I joined the workforce at age 15…



And after graduating high school, I worked at quite a few different jobs…



But eventually I ended up at Costco. 



Now, Costco seemed like a great place to work.



They tell you when you join that you'll get plenty of opportunities to climb up the corporate ladder. 



They hire internally, you'll receive health benefits, and eventually if you stick with them long enough, you could have a management position making high five figures per year.



For a kid who was just coming out of high school, this sounded like an amazing opportunity. 



So every day I clocked in, I gave them my best. 



I have always taken pride in working my hardest and being a model employee. 



I never called in sick. I would work any shift they gave me without complaining...



Even working late-night closing shifts every single weekend. 



In fact, I was one of their fastest cashiers. 



And because of that, they always scheduled me on their busiest days. 



I thought if they can see my work ethic and responsibility as an employee…



The next time a supervision position opens up, I'll be a shoe in and my salary will jump. 



But the longer I worked at Costco, the more I realized that it didn't matter how hard I worked. 



The system wasn’t set up to reward their best workers. 



Instead, every time a management spot opened up, they would always pick the person who had been working there the longest…



Despite the fact that I worked circles around those same people.



This really upset me because here I was giving it my all...



Working harder than everyone and clearly one of the top performers...



But to my managers, I was just another gear in the machine. 



It was apparent that it would take me years and years to get where I wanted to be…



And even worse, it was almost completely out of my control. 



My breaking point came when I went out to the parking lot to gather up all the carts. 



I spent nearly an hour collecting all of them by myself in the pouring rain…



And when I walked back into the store soaking wet from head to toe..



The new manager that I got passed over for wrote me up for having an “unprofessional appearance” on the job. 



At that moment…I broke. 



I got so frustrated because here I am working my heart out, giving it on my all every day. 



But no matter how much effort I put in, it wouldn’t get me anywhere. 



I knew there had to be another way. 



I didn't know what it was, but I knew I had to make something happen. 



Otherwise I'd never be able to do things I dreamed about all the time. 



Things like traveling  the world, buying a home, pursuing other interests. 



So over the next few years I tried several different business models. 



I got my real estate license. 



I tried reselling products online. 



I got involved in the stock market. 



However, these business models had been around a super long time. 



And so the competition was extremely difficult. 



Then one day I stumbled across a newer business model: private label.



Let me explain exactly how this unique business model works with private label. 



You have the opportunity to create your own brand made up of one or more physical products. 



You're able to work with manufacturers from all over the world to make products. 



And now there's absolutely no middleman. 



Remember Sean at the beginning, this is exactly what he did. 



What I love is you don't have to reinvent the wheel or become some kind of inventor. 



With the right research methods, you can find out exactly what sells well on Amazon...



And then reach out to a manufacturer to help produce it for extremely low prices. 



Imagine being able to source high quality merchandise and products at the same prices companies like Walmart and Target do...



And then put your own brand logo on it and sell it online. 



And since you're working directly with the supplier manufacturer...



You have rock bottom pricing, which allows for extremely high margins.



I've seen margins higher than a hundred percent return on investment.



Imagine that you put a hundred dollars in and get back over $200 back...



And you rinse and repeat that over and over and over again. 



And this process of private label works flawlessly with Amazon.



Let me show you how first you find a product. 



We do this by looking at what's already selling well on Amazon. 



With a few unique tools I use, I'm able to see exactly how much every product is selling per month on Amazon. 



Once we find a product that shows clear demand...



We then reach out to a supplier who nine out of 10 times already makes that exact product. 



After receiving samples of the product, we're then able to negotiate payment terms. 



I'll touch more on this a little bit later...



We do this through a secure site, just like PayPal, where we're fully covered...



And have an inspector visit the factory to hand check all of our products before shipping them to Amazon.



We work with professional overseas shipping companies (think FedEx or UPS)...



But specializing in overseas shipments and having it sent to an Amazon warehouse.  



After the product arrives at the Amazon warehouse, we upload photos in a description for our product....



And then run some advertising on Amazon so the product can show on the front page of search results and be seen by the tremendous amount of customers hungry to buy. 



Once a sale is made, Amazon takes care of everything else. 



We literally start being able to make money while we sleep without anything but a laptop and an internet connection. 



So this entire process lets you leverage all of the power behind Amazon in a few ways. 



One, you're using Amazon's warehouses, meaning you don't have to worry about storing all of those products in your garage or your room.



Two, you're using Amazon's platform, which is the number one website people go to when they want to buy a product. 



And three, you're using the Amazon shipping system, so they pack your products and ship them out for you...



Meaning you don't have to go to the post office and waste time waiting in a line. 



This is the fulfillment by Amazon program or FBA for short. 



And when you pair FBA with the private label business model, you're able to scale your business to the moon and keep incredible profit margins. 



Now, after I honed this process to a T...



I got to the point where I was routinely shipping out full containers' worth of my products from overseas.

Okay? So let's get down to business for this part of the presentation. 



I highly recommend you take out a pin or pencil and something to take notes on...



Because I want to show you the fastest and most powerful way to start making money from Amazon. 



So let let's dive in.



This system I've developed to help me and so many others win on Amazon is called compound layering. 



Remember the FBA process I just showed you? 



Well, there's six parts to launching a product on Amazon. 



One: finding the product...



Two, sourcing the product...



Three, shipping the product...



Four: listing the product...



Five: advertising the product...



And six: launching the product. 



And each of these six parts play a pivotal role in your product being successful or not. 



Also each part has a level at which it can be executed from beginner to intermediate to advanced, which is the highest level expert, 



Almost all new sellers on Amazon execute these six parts at the beginner level at a pure inexperience.



And honestly you don't know what you don't know. 



Compound layering utilizes every trick, tip, and method I know from years in this business, so you can execute each of these six parts at an expert level. 



And when all six parts are executed at an expert level, the competition doesn't stand a chance.



You see, when you use compound layering to sell products on Amazon, you gain an unfair advantage. 



In many areas, you're able to find better and more profitable products to sell on Amazon...



You're able to build better relationships with your manufacturers overseas, which is the key to getting better pricing and higher quality service...



And you're able to get buyers on Amazon picking your products over one else and having them click that buy now right away.



Now remember how I said earlier that I could explain to you why compound layering works so well using pancakes?



You see the pancakes on the left. 



That's most sellers on Amazon. 



They don't really try all that hard. 



They put the minimum amount of effort into all parts of that six point FBA process. 



And even the ones that do put in a lot of effort, they lack any real world experience in this business. 



And the result is what you see on the left, something no one really wants, unless they're absolutely starving. 



Or in this case of buying a product, they need it right now and there's no other options. 



But someone who utilizes compound layering, they're more like the pancakes on the right...



People pick their product over everyone else's every single time. 



I think the best way to explain compound layering is by using the process I just showed you on how Amazon FBA works (those six steps)...



And then showing you what an average seller does versus what my students and I do to utilize comp and layering and stand out from all of our competition on Amazon.

It all starts with finding products to sell on Amazon. Step one of that six part process. 



Now, most sellers just look at what other people are selling and copy that product. 



Exactly. Like take a look at this door handle. 



Most sellers would just copy this exact product, creating what's known as a me too product. 



Everyone has it and all they do is compete on price and until they lower it. 



So it's making no money. 



But sellers who leverage compound layering think: "how can I make this product better?"



They look at things like the frequently bought together section on Amazon...



And they see that when people buy the door handle, they're often buying a latch to go with it. 



So they create a bundle using both of the items, which allows 'em to charge more and get more purchases.



The second part of the process has to do with who you're getting your products from or "sourcing your products."



Step number two, in that six step process. 



And like we said, most of the time we're going to be getting those products from a manufacturer overseas, such as China.



Normal sellers conduct business like they do in the US. 



They think about it like this: "I pay you, you send me the product, that's it." 



But sellers who understand compound layering know that, in Chinese culture, business relationships and personal relationships are extremely intertwined...



They even have a name for this in China. It's called "Quanxi." 



Essentially, Quanxi requires two parties to share a personal bond before any business relationship can develop. 



And by developing personal relationships with these manufacturers, you'll always be in a better position to make more money than someone who lacks it.



After years of trial and error, I discovered that gift giving is one of the best ways to create that bond and facilitate a business relationship. 



Let me show you an example. 



A few years ago, I was trying to work with this new manufacturer and found out the owner had a favorite basketball player: Vince Carter. 



Knowing this, I went on eBay, bought a basketball signed by Vince Carter and had it shipped to my manufacturer. 



As a result, my prices dropped, my service increased, and I had a better relationship with this manufacturer. 



And when you have a better relationship with your manufacturers, you're not only going to be able to make more money because they'll lower their price...



But also whenever there's a time crunch and the factory is super busy, you often get priority over everyone else.



So you've seen the difference between how normal sellers and my students and I find our products...



And how we deal with manufacturers to create better relationships and secure the best possible deals...



Now let's show you the difference on how we list the products on Amazon to sell...



Compared to normal sellers, who follow a DIY or super low budget approach to everything they do...



And as a result, their product packaging usually comes in a blank box...



Their product logo looks terrible because they made it for free online or paid $5 for it on Upwork...



And then they themselves throw together a poorly written product description and title that fails to sell anyone. 



But my students and I, we do the exact opposite.

 

We hire the best of the best in each area of the product listing....



That means we hire professional photographers, professional package designers, professional logo designers, and professional marketing writers. 



In fact, I let my students have access to my own Rolodex of contacts for each of these specialists. 



The very best of the best in the world. 



As a result, our listings look so much better and they easily attract more attention than every single other product listing...



Because the difference in quality is easily visible. 



People don't wanna buy something that has terrible pictures, horrible descriptions, and looks cheap.



Sure, everybody loves a good deal, but cheap means probably going to break or not work...



Which means they'll have to buy it again from someone else. 



So consumers will gladly pay more for your product simply because the presentation is that much better. 



In short, most sellers on Amazon are just doing the bare minimum with their products...



But someone who understands compound layering makes sure all aspects of their Amazon business are top notch. 



Those six different areas are expert level. And as a result, they see way better results. 



Again, going back to those pancakes, will some people pick the ones on the left? Sure. 



But it's only going to be when they don't have the pancakes on the right as another option.



Now these are just three areas where utilizing compound layering can put you light years ahead of everyone else selling on Amazon.

Allowing us to profitably take the number one spot in search results so that everyone can find our products...



Or the useful little secret we use to turn a customer into a repeat buyer, allowing us to double, triple or even quadruple our profit per customer. 



Not to mention all the methods I discovered on how to grow your brand off of Amazon... 



So you can make money without having to pay Amazon anything...

So we can enjoy more profit and not unnecessarily waste thousands of dollars on every shipment
